rulings.Case Title: 415 TWENTY SEVENTH STREET LLC. VS. JOHN KAHLER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Friday November 04, 2019 Line 3. DEFENDANT JOHN KAHLER, CHARLENE KAHLER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Ellis Act allows for termination of tenancies subject to landlord's compliance with all of its provisions. One of the requirements of Ellis Act/SFRO is a delivery of NOI, which triggers the calculation of the termination date. NOI on which the termination date is purportedly based failed to comply with requirements under any standard, strict or substantial. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that is situations where a declaration is required, an undeclared statement would be legally sufficient. To the extent Plaintiff is relying on the new NOI as a triggering document, the issue of its validity is not before the Court as the new NOI does not form the basis for this unlawful detainer and any action based on the new NOI would be admittedly premature at this time. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B as no courtesy copy of the supplemental brief has been provided. Failure to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future will result in briefs not being considered. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 415 TWENTY SEVENTH STREET LLC. VS. WILLIAM KAHLER ET AL
